title: World War II
summary: The deadliest conflict in history — how Hitler's lightning war conquered Europe, why it finally turned, and how the world fought back.

context.headline: Lightning war, then a world in flames
context.description: In September 1939 Germany unleashed Blitzkrieg — 'lightning war' — on Poland, and within a year had conquered most of Europe. Only Britain held out across the Channel. Then Hitler gambled everything on invading the Soviet Union, and Japan dragged in the United States. What began in Europe became a truly global war fought across the world's continents and oceans.
